solution:
def lengthOfLongestSubstring(s: str) -> int:
    n = len(s)
    max_length = 0
    start = 0
    seen = {}

    for i in range(n):
        char = s[i]
        if char in seen and start < seen[char]:
            start = seen[char] + 1
        seen[char] = i + 1
        max_length = max(max_length, i - start + 1)

    return max_length

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The problem requires finding the length of the longest substring without duplicate characters. We use a dictionary (seen) to keep track of the last occurrence of each character. We iterate through the string and update the start index when a duplicate character is encountered. Finally, we return the maximum length between the current substring and the previous substring. This solution has a time complexity of O(n) and a space complexity of O(n), where n is the length of the string.
